concepts.title: ZPLS Node Group
concepts.summary: A node group is a collection of up to 20 ZPLS modules that together support up to 100,000 users. Node groups can enable redundancy, where backup nodes take over if primary nodes fail. Designers should note that node groups are the top-level organizational unit for ZPLS capacity planning.
key_points: Up to 20 ZPLS modules per node group
Supports up to 100,000 users (50,000 with redundancy)
Nodes must be unassigned (not tied to a site or other group) to be added

concepts.title: ZPLS Redundancy
concepts.summary: Redundancy ensures continuous operation if individual ZPLS modules fail. Backup nodes must be configured at a 2:1 ratio (2 primary : 1 backup). This ratio constraint is a design-relevant rule that should be surfaced during node group setup.
key_points: Enabled at node group creation time
2:1 primary-to-backup node ratio required
Redundancy halves effective user capacity (50k vs 100k)

concepts.title: ZPLS Round-Robin User Distribution
concepts.summary: When a node group is assigned to a site, users and common areas are automatically distributed across ZPLS nodes using round-robin. This is fully automated and invisible to admins — no manual control is exposed in the admin portal.
key_points: Automatic round-robin distribution across nodes in group
No manual control or override available in admin UI
Transparent to end users

user_roles.title: Account Owner or Admin with Edit Privileges
user_roles.summary: Node group creation, assignment, and management require admin privileges. The same role also performs ZPLS validation through the Zoom client's Statistics > Phone tab — this is a rare case where admin actions span both web portal and desktop client.
key_points: Full node group management via web portal
Validation requires checking desktop client Statistics > Phone tab
Must have account settings edit privilege

task_flows.title: Create ZPLS Node Group
task_flows.summary: Admin navigates: Phone System Management > Company Info > Account Settings > Zoom Node > Local Survivability Manage > Node Groups > New. Key decision points: naming, enabling redundancy toggle, and selecting unassigned nodes from a list. UX note: only unassigned nodes appear in the selection list — this constraint should be communicated inline.
key_points: Path: Phone System Management > Company Info > Account Settings > Zoom Node > Manage > Node Groups > New
Naming, redundancy toggle, node selection are the core inputs
Only unassigned nodes appear in selection list

task_flows.title: Assign Node Group to Site and Route Group
task_flows.summary: From the Node Groups tab, admin clicks '...' > Edit on a node group, selects Site and Route Group, then saves. The site assignment triggers automatic round-robin user distribution.
key_points: Access via Node Groups tab > '...' menu > Edit
Two selections: Site and Route Group
Auto-triggers round-robin user distribution on save

task_flows.title: Validate ZPLS Configuration on Zoom Client
task_flows.summary: Admin validates ZPLS by opening desktop client: profile > Settings > Statistics > Phone tab. Status shows 'Configured' initially, then 'Registered' once client successfully registers with ZPLS. This is a verification step, not configuration.
key_points: Client-side validation path: Settings > Statistics > Phone
Status transitions: Configured → Registered
IP address also displayed for verification

constraints.title: Node Group Redundancy Ratio
constraints.summary: Backup nodes must be at a 2:1 ratio to primary nodes. This is a hard constraint that should be enforced or warned about during node group setup — adding nodes without correct backup ratio should trigger guidance.
key_points: 2 primary : 1 backup ratio required
Affects user capacity calculation

constraints.title: Unassigned Node Requirement
constraints.summary: Only ZPLS modules that are not already assigned to a site or node group can be added to a new node group. The selection UI should filter out already-assigned nodes rather than showing them as disabled.
key_points: Nodes must be unassigned to a site or existing node group
Max 20 modules per group

ux_patterns.title: Progressive Setup Wizard Pattern
ux_patterns.summary: ZPLS node group setup follows a multi-step flow: deploy modules → create node group → assign to site → validate on client. Each step depends on the previous, making this a natural wizard pattern with clear dependency indicators.
key_points: Sequential dependency chain: deploy → group → assign → validate
Each step has its own admin portal section
Validation step crosses from web portal to desktop client
